hero.title: Rehabilitation with precision in every movement.
hero.description: uFlex helps physiotherapists monitor exercises with inertial sensors, kinematic analysis, and actionable metrics to speed up patient recovery.
hero.imageAlt: Patient using the uFlex device during a rehabilitation session

card.title: Biomechanical data ready to interpret.

sync.title: Continuous synchronization

how.title: A simple experience for the therapist, sophisticated underneath.
how.description: From placing the device to analyzing rehabilitation, uFlex supports the entire clinical flow with a clear and actionable interface.

placement.title: Placement
placement.description: The wearable is adjusted on the target joint to start a stable capture.

sync.title: Synchronization
sync.description: The app pairs the sensor, calibrates movement, and prepares the clinical reading.

faq.title: Quick answers for clinical teams and technology partners.

evaluation.question: Does uFlex replace the physiotherapist's evaluation?
evaluation.answer: No. uFlex complements clinical judgment with objective measurements to support follow-up and decision-making.

exercises.question: What types of exercises can it monitor?
exercises.answer: It is oriented toward mobility and joint-control exercises where orientation and range of motion are relevant variables.

internet.question: Is a permanent internet connection required?
internet.answer: Capture can operate locally depending on the implemented workflow; report synchronization can be performed later.

sensors.question: How are the BNO055 sensors integrated?
sensors.answer: The sensors are mounted on the wearable and provide fused orientation data that is then transformed into kinematic metrics useful for the app.

battery.question: Is the battery included with the device?
battery.answer: Yes. uFlex includes an integrated battery in the kit to ensure autonomy and portability during rehabilitation sessions.
